title: Generated Adapter Drift And Conformance
goal: Implement #360 by making generated CLI adapter freshness, contract backing, and black-box behavior part of ordinary check/conformance tooling.
non_goals: Implement #361 no-direct-edit guardrails beyond the checks needed for #360.

intent.outcome: Generated adapter output can be deterministically checked, contract-backed, and behaviorally exercised through ordinary validation surfaces.
intent.constraints: Stay on master; keep checks repo-/agent-/tool-/language-agnostic in package logic; keep runtime primitive behavior hand-written.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest check/test wiring that distinguishes contract, generator, generated adapter, and runtime behavior failures.
intent.escalation: Stop before broad generated adapter policy, package CLI generation, or direct-edit enforcement that belongs to #361.

execution.next_step: Archive this slice and continue with #361 no-direct-edit guardrails.
execution.proof: Use proof --changed, generator freshness, contract tooling checks, conformance tests, workspace CLI tests, ruff, planning checks, reconcile, summary.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Generated shipped CLI adapters from authoritative package definitions.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After generated-adapter drift/conformance checks are in ordinary validation.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Generated adapters become safer to expand because stale output and behavior drift are caught by checks.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: No-direct-edit enforcement, planning/memory package adapter generation, and multi-adapter projection requirements.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The generated adapter output check is now part of contract-tooling validation; #361 can focus on direct-edit guardrails instead of freshness.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Contract tooling fails on stale generated adapter output and generated adapters are tied to black-box conformance contracts.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: #361 should prevent or flag manual edits to generated CLI surfaces.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: #361 no-direct-edit guardrails for generated CLI surfaces.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Continue implementing planned work until done.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Proceed from #359 into #360 using the first generated defaults adapter as the conformance target.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Make generated defaults adapter freshness and behavior visible in ordinary checks/tests.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check failure messages make the failing layer clear: contract, generator, generated output, or runtime primitive.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: conformance/check tooling, generator freshness surfaces, focused tests, planning state/evidence/review artifacts.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: proof --changed; generator freshness; check_contract_tooling_surfaces; generated conformance tests; workspace CLI tests; ruff; planning checks; reconcile; summary.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Any broad conformance framework rewrite or new permanent first-line surface.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Planning/memory package CLIs, direct-edit enforcement policy, multi-adapter projection requirements.

stop_conditions.stop when: #360 is closed, committed, pushed, and #361 remains planned.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The check requires broad CLI architecture changes.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: Freshness, contract, conformance, or runtime behavior checks fail.

context_budget.recoverable later: Direct-edit guardrails and package adapter generation.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run and closeout_distillation before archive.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Add generated CLI adapter drift and conformance checks.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: No new adapters; no runtime primitive migration; checks must stay package-agnostic.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact check/test location and failure-message shape.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The change becomes a broader generation architecture refactor.

immediate_next_action: Inspect existing conformance and contract-tooling checks for #360 insertion points.
invariants: Do not make generated adapters own runtime primitive behavior.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Generated adapter freshness should be checked from the authoritative contract/generator path, not by manually asserting generated file details only.
open_questions_to_close: Where should ordinary drift checks live so CI catches stale generated adapter output?
Which black-box conformance scenario proves the generated defaults adapter path?
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_generated_tool_conformance.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py
uv run ruff check src tests scripts
completion_criteria: Generated adapter output freshness is part of ordinary checks.
At least one generated adapter has black-box conformance coverage.
Failure messages distinguish the likely failing layer.
No active execplan remains after archive closeout.

execution_run.what happened: Added generated adapter output freshness/parity checking to contract-tooling validation and tied generated adapters to black-box process conformance contracts.
execution_run.scope touched: Contract tooling check, generated tool conformance tests, planning state/evidence/review artifacts.
execution_run.validations run: proof --changed; generator freshness; check_contract_tooling_surfaces; generated conformance and contract tests; workspace CLI tests; ruff; planning doctor; planning surface check; reconcile; summary.
execution_run.result for continuation: #360 closed; #361 remains open for no-direct-edit guardrails.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; no new adapters or runtime primitive migration.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes for #360; no for parent #356, which continues through #361-#363.
finished_run_review.config compliance: dogfooded preflight, proof, generator freshness, contract tooling, conformance tests, summary, reconcile, doctor, and planning checks.

proof_report.proof achieved now: generated output freshness, contract-tooling drift, black-box conformance coverage, workspace CLI behavior, lint, and planning health all passed.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py checks generated adapter output; tests/test_generated_tool_conformance.py asserts generated adapters have process conformance contracts.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Continue implementing planned work until done.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: #360 added generated adapter freshness and black-box conformance guardrails

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Move shipped CLI adapter interface authority behind implementation-independent contracts while preserving runtime primitive ownership.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Make the first generated adapter trustworthy before expanding coverage.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can stale generated adapter output and runtime drift be caught by ordinary validation?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #360 delivered drift/conformance checks; parent #356 still needs no-direct-edit guardrails and adapter expansion.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen #360 if generated adapter output can drift from command_adapter_generation.json without contract-tooling failure.
